name: Cream Puff Swans
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in water and bring to a boil.
2. Sift flour with salt and add to the butter mixture. Heat, stirring, until the mixture forms a ball.
3. Remove from heat and add eggs, one at a time, beating well between each addition.
4. Put 1/3 of the dough into a pastry bag and pipe through the largest hole onto a greased baking sheet, forming six S-shaped pastries, each about 3 inches long. These will form the necks and heads of swans.
5. Scrape any remaining dough from the pastry bag into the rest of the dough.
6. On a separate, greased baking sheet, form the remaining dough into six ovals, drawing the dough to a point at one end and rounding the other end of the oval. These will be the bodies of swans.
7. Place the baking sheet with oval cream puffs in the oven at 425 degrees and bake for 10 minutes. Place the other baking sheet in the oven and continue to bake both batches of dough another 10 minutes.
8. Turn the oven down to 350 degrees and bake an additional 5 minutes, or until the heads of the swans are done. Bake the bodies 5-10 minutes longer, or until they are done.
9. As the baking sheets are removed from the oven, remove pastries to racks to cool completely.
10. Cut an oval from the top of each body and cut in half. Scoop any uncooked dough from the bodies.
11. Whip cream until stiff. Beat in powdered sugar to taste.
12. Put a spoonful of well-drained Mandarin orange slices into each body, dividing equally among the six.
13. Top with equal amounts of whipped cream. Place the S-shapes at the rounded ends to form necks and heads.
14. Place half-ovals behind the "necks" to form wings.
15. Serve immediately.
